product.name: ArWoWear T30 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, Flame Retardant Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T30 Flame Pro is light weight 260 g/m², 90% cotton 10% polyamide, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, Flame Retardant Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
260 g/m², 90% Cotton 10% Polyamide, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1200N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>25N; Weft>20N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612
functionIconLabels: >> ArWoWear T30 Flame Pro Data Sheet

acceptedMatches.rationale: Fabric is explicitly certified to EN ISO 11611 for welding protection and molten-metal splash resistance, directly indicating welding/metalworking application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: EN 61482 arc protection and ATPV rating are present, but these relate to arc-flash protective clothing generally; no explicit statement of electrical utility or energy-sector use.
EN ISO 11612 (flame and heat protection) is present, but this standard covers general industrial heat/flame protection, not specifically firefighter turnout gear; no explicit firefighting use stated.

primary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification plus molten-metal splash and arc-flash protection make welding/metalworking the most specific and defensible primary application.

evidence: EN ISO 11612 flame and heat protection
secondary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly positioned as general FR workwear/coveralls fabric with broad industrial heat and flame protection certification (EN ISO 11612), supporting a broader workwear-industrial use alongside its welding-specific certification.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Arc protection (EN 61482) is present but no explicit electrical utility or energy-sector use stated; arc protection here is tied to welding hazard, not electrical work.
EN ISO 11612 covers general flame/heat protection, not specifically firefighter turnout gear; no firefighting-specific claim.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Arc protection (EN 61482) is present but no explicit electrical utility or energy-sector use stated; arc protection here is tied to welding hazard, not electrical work.
EN ISO 11612 covers general flame/heat protection, not specifically firefighter turnout gear; no firefighting-specific claim.

normalized.reviewReason: Secondary candidate did not pass confidence threshold 70.
